uk: 25🕂 Успіння святої Анни, матері Пресвятої Богородиці
Пам'ять святих жінок Олімпіади і Євпраксії
saints.en: Dormition of St. Anna, Mother of the Most Holy Theotokos. Commemoration of Sts. Women Olympias & Eupraxia

rubrics:
Вечірня: „Блажен муж”. На „Господи, взиваю” – 6 стихир св. Анни, Слава, І нині: богородичний-догмат 7-го гласу. Прокімен дня, читань нема. Стиховня ціла св. Анни, зі своїми приспівами. Слава, І нині: св. Анни. Тропар св. Анни (без богородичного). Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби св. Анни.
Утреня: по „Бог Господь”: тропар св. Анни (2), Слава, І нині: ще раз. Катизми чергові з сідальними св. Анни, також полієлей з величаннями і пополієлейний сідальний св. Анни. Степенна 4-го голосу, перший антифон. Прокімен св. Анни. Єв. – Мт. зач. 56; 13, 54-58. Пс. 50, і стихира св. Анни. Канон Богородиці і св. Анни. По 3-ій пісні – сідальний св. Анни. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос св. Анни. По 9-ій – світильний св. Анни. Хвалитні стихири св. Анни – 4, Слава, І нині: св. Анни. По великому славослов'ї з трисвятим – тропар св. Анни (без богородичного). Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби св. Анни.
Часи: на всіх – тропар і кондак св. Анни.
Літургія: все – тільки св. Анни. Апостол і євангелія спочатку. Ряд.: Ап. – Рим. 113 зач.; 14, 6-9. Єв. – Мт. 64 зач.; 15, 32-39. Анни: Ап. – Гал. 210 зач.; 4, 22-31. Єв. – Лк. 36 зач.; 8, 16-21.

troparion_en.text: Divinely-wise Anna, you carried in your womb the pure Mother of God, who gave life to our Life. / Therefore, you are now carried joyfully to the inheritance of heaven, / to the abode of those who rejoice in glory, / where you seek forgiveness of sins for those who faithfully honor you, ever blessed one.

kontakion_en.text: We celebrate the memory of the progenitors of Christ, / and with faith we ask their help, / that deliverance from every affliction be granted to those who cry out: / “Be with us, O God, who in Your good pleasure glorified them.”

matched_oca_saint: Dormition of the Righteous Anna, the Mother of the Most Holy Theotokos

oca_saints.name: Dormition of the Righteous Anna, the Mother of the Most Holy Theotokos

troparion.text: Divinely-wise Anna, you carried in your womb the pure Mother of God, who gave life to our Life. / Therefore, you are now carried joyfully to the inheritance of heaven, / to the abode of those who rejoice in glory, / where you seek forgiveness of sins for those who faithfully honor you, ever blessed one.

kontakion.text: We celebrate the memory of the progenitors of Christ, / and with faith we ask their help, / that deliverance from every affliction be granted to those who cry out: / “Be with us, O God, who in Your good pleasure glorified them.”

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Joseph was amazed...”)
Your lamb Eupraxia, / calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / “I love You, my Bridegroom, / and in seeking You, I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love.” / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Finding your most revered temple to be a source of healing for our souls,  / we the faithful cry out to You with a loud voice:  / “O most renowned Virgin Martyr Eupraxia,  / entreat Christ God unceasingly on behalf of us all.”

oca_saints.name: Venerable Makarios, Abbot of Zheltovod and Unzha

troparion.text: Today your city of Galich praises you, for within its boundaries you shone as a great sun;  / and by your miracles, O Venerable Makarios, you have illumined all things.  / Now pray to the Lord that our souls may be delivered from the temptations of the Enemy,  / and that your city may be protected from the invasion of pagans.

kontakion.text: You appeared as a second Moses, O Venerable one, for he divided the sea with his staff,  / and you conquered the passions like Amalek,1 passing through the impenetrable wilderness with an untroubled mind.  / There, by your prayers, you worked great miracles, feeding hungry people to satiety.  / Now pray to the Lord to give consolation to all those in sorrow, O ever-memorable Father Makarios,  / and to the region of Galich, and praise and affirmation to all the Russian land.
